name: Boundary Refactor Wave J — Chat Persistence
outcome_goal: All writes currently in runtime/chat_orchestrator.py are moved into storage/postgres/*_repository.py modules; no INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE and no CREATE TABLE remain in those files; any inline DDL is in a migration.
anti_requirements: Do NOT change the public API of the runtime modules — existing callers keep working
Do NOT alter table schemas. This is a move, not a redesign.
Do NOT widen writes outside storage/postgres/ and Code&DBs/Databases/migrations/workflow/

jobs.label: wave_j_plan
jobs.outcome_goal: A written plan names every repository/authority method, every migration, and every callsite
jobs.prompt:
You are planning Wave J — Chat Persistence. Do not write code yet.

Linked bugs:
- BUG-AD0DB73979AC — runtime/chat_orchestrator.py: conversations + conversation_messages + tool_result_traces

Sources with leaked authority:
- Code&DBs/Workflow/runtime/chat_orchestrator.py

Target destinations:
- storage/postgres/chat_repository.py

Read each source file fully and produce a written plan naming:
1. Every method signature for every new/extended repository or authority module
2. Which tables already have migrations and which need a new migration file (name the number)
3. Every callsite that will need follow-up changes
4. The exact grep-based regression check each downstream job must pass

Reference idiom: storage/postgres/memory_graph_repository.py, storage/postgres/workflow_runtime_repository.py, storage/postgres/validators.py.

jobs.label: wave_j_migrations
jobs.outcome_goal: No CREATE TABLE remains in source files; any missing table has a migration
jobs.prompt:
For Wave J, audit every source file for inline CREATE TABLE or CREATE INDEX statements. For each one, either confirm it has a matching migration under Code&DBs/Databases/migrations/workflow/ or add a new migration with the next available number. Match columns and constraints exactly — this is a move, not a redesign. Do NOT edit the source files yet.

Sources:
- Code&DBs/Workflow/runtime/chat_orchestrator.py
jobs.verify_command: cd /Users/nate/Praxis && PYTHONPATH='Code&DBs/Workflow' /opt/homebrew/bin/python3 -c 'from storage.postgres import ensure_postgres_available; ensure_postgres_available()'

jobs.label: wave_j_create_repositories
jobs.outcome_goal: Every needed repository/authority class exists with typed methods and package exports
jobs.prompt:
Create the repository/authority modules named in the Wave J plan. Mirror the idiom from storage/postgres/memory_graph_repository.py: class Postgres<Domain>Repository, __init__(conn), explicit method names, input validation via storage.postgres.validators.

Cover every INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE currently in these source files:
- Code&DBs/Workflow/runtime/chat_orchestrator.py

Target files to create:
- storage/postgres/chat_repository.py

Export new classes from Code&DBs/Workflow/storage/postgres/__init__.py so downstream packages can import them. Do NOT edit the source files yet — the thinning step happens next.
jobs.verify_command: cd /Users/nate/Praxis && PYTHONPATH='Code&DBs/Workflow' /opt/homebrew/bin/python3 -c 'from storage.postgres import ensure_postgres_available; ensure_postgres_available()'

jobs.label: wave_j_thin_sources
jobs.description: Thin the source files to delegate through repositories
jobs.outcome_goal: No SQL or CREATE TABLE remains in any source file; public APIs unchanged
jobs.prompt:
Rewrite each source file to delegate every write to the repositories/authority modules created in the previous job. Keep public function and class signatures unchanged. Remove every SQL string and every CREATE TABLE.

Sources to thin:
- Code&DBs/Workflow/runtime/chat_orchestrator.py

For each source file, verify with:
```
! grep -E 'INSERT |UPDATE |DELETE FROM|CREATE TABLE' <file>
python3 -m py_compile <file>
```
jobs.verify_command: cd /Users/nate/Praxis && PYTHONPATH='Code&DBs/Workflow' /opt/homebrew/bin/python3 -m py_compile 'Code&DBs/Workflow/runtime/chat_orchestrator.py' && ! grep -rE 'INSERT |UPDATE |DELETE FROM|CREATE TABLE' 'Code&DBs/Workflow/runtime/chat_orchestrator.py'

jobs.label: wave_j_tests
jobs.description: Prove the migration with tests and regression assertions
jobs.outcome_goal: Round-trip tests + grep regression tests pass; existing suites stay green
jobs.prompt:
Add or extend tests for Wave J.

Steps:
1. Run existing tests under Code&DBs/Workflow/tests/ that exercise the source files.
2. For each new repository or authority module, add at least one round-trip test.
3. Add a single regression test file that opens each source file as text and asserts it contains no 'INSERT ', 'UPDATE ', 'DELETE FROM', or 'CREATE TABLE' substrings.
4. Run the relevant test subset.

jobs.label: wave_j_close_bugs
jobs.description: Resolve the wave's linked bugs as FIXED
jobs.outcome_goal: Every linked bug is FIXED with a resolution citing this queue_id
jobs.prompt:
Resolve the Wave J linked bugs as FIXED after regression tests pass.

```bash
cd /Users/nate/Praxis
. ./scripts/_workflow_env.sh && workflow_load_repo_env
"Code&DBs/Workflow/workflow" tools call praxis_bugs --input-json '{
  "command": "resolve",
  "bug_id": "BUG-AD0DB73979AC",
  "status": "FIXED",
  "resolution": "Writes moved out of runtime/chat_orchestrator.py in boundary_refactor_wave_j_chat_20260413. Verified no SQL/CREATE TABLE remains."
}' --yes
```

jobs.label: wave_j_review
jobs.description: Independent review of the wave rewrite
jobs.outcome_goal: Any remaining leaks, stale DDL, or missed callsites are called out in writing
jobs.prompt:
Review the Wave J rewrite as a code review.

Prioritize findings on:
- any residual SQL strings or CREATE TABLE in the source files
- callsites that relied on exception types/return shapes that changed
- lost observability or silenced errors in the new repositories/authority modules
- missed or weak tests around upsert semantics and error paths
- whether new classes are exported from storage/postgres/__init__.py

Findings first. If no findings remain, say so explicitly.
